hero.question: Israel strike on Yemen by March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing in a roughly 1-in-3 chance Israel strikes Yemen by end of Q1 2026, which assumes escalation picks up significantly from the current low-intensity exchange of mostly intercepted drone/missile volleys. The 4-point overnight jump suggests new intel or rhetoric shifted sentiment, but the Houthis have absorbed months of strikes without triggering a full Israeli campaign, and 14 months is a long time for this conflict to stay at peak intensity without resolution or de-escalation.

daily_take.headline: Yemen strike odds spike 4 points. Market pricing in escalation that hasn't happened yet.
daily_take.deck: Israel strike on Yemen jumped to 34.5% overnight on fresh rhetoric about regional threats. The move feels reactive to chatter, not actual military posture. Smart money sees 2025 as the window for this action, not 2026, which makes the current price a fade opportunity for bears.

sidebar.headline: Iran entry odds flatlined at 5.2% despite Yemen escalation talk. Market treating these as independent events when they're clearly correlated. Asymmetric risk here if regional tensions spike.
sidebar.label: US-Iran conflict: 5.2%

sidebar.headline: Arizona jumped 2.2 points to 36.3%. Check if Vegas sharp money moved on recent tournament performance or if this is retail chasing a March Madness narrative.
sidebar.label: Arizona NCAA win: 36.3%

sidebar.headline: Trump removal market dead at 6.5%. Tiny volume signals consensus pricing. Nothing to do here unless fundamentals crack.
sidebar.label: Trump out by August 2026: 6.5%
